title: Don't read full VM File before decompressing
body:
While working on #21592 we figured out that the full VM File was loaded in memory when detecting the file format, but only a few bytes are needed. This commit addresses that.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```

comments.body:
> Note in general we prefer the commits to be squashed when addressing code review comments. `Fixup after mtrmac review` isn't really a helpful commit message once merged into main.

For confirmation: preferred way is to sqash + push force right? So even if you lose track of what has been updated in the PR, the commit history is ready for main and doesn't need to be squashed.

Yes (or, optionally, you can create semantically-meaningful multiple commits, and maintain that with `git rebase -i`).

GitHub is making a record of force-pushes in the PR; the record is imperfect but typically good enough to use for keeping up in reviews.

The workflow of adding commits on top of the previous version of a PR doesn’t work if the previous version does not compile at all — the “build each commit” test fails; and we have that test to increase the utility of `git bisect` to work. So force pushes need to be an option, in principle.

> Yes (or, optionally, you can create semantically-meaningful multiple commits, and maintain that with `git rebase -i`).
> 
> GitHub is making a record of force-pushes in the PR; the record is imperfect but typically good enough to use for keeping up in reviews.
> 
> The workflow of adding commits on top of the previous version of a PR doesn’t work if the previous version does not compile at all — the “build each commit” test fails; and we have that test to increase the utility of `git bisect` to work. So force pushes need to be an option, in principle.

Fair enough

```
--> copying "C:\\Users\\ADMINI~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman-machine-daily.x86_64.hyperv.vhdx" to "C:\\Users\\ADMINI~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman_test3454669177\\podman-machine-daily.x86_64.hyperv.vhdx"/nfailed to close destination file "C:\\Users\\ADMINI~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman_test3454669177\\podman-machine-daily.x86_64.hyperv.vhdx": %!q(<nil>)
```

Error seems legitimate

> ```
> --> copying "C:\\Users\\ADMINI~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman-machine-daily.x86_64.hyperv.vhdx" to "C:\\Users\\ADMINI~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman_test3454669177\\podman-machine-daily.x86_64.hyperv.vhdx"/nfailed to close destination file "C:\\Users\\ADMINI~1\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman_test3454669177\\podman-machine-daily.x86_64.hyperv.vhdx": %!q(<nil>)
> ```
> 
> Error seems legitimate

I am not able to reproduce this error. On my windows 11 with Hyper-V `.\bin\windows\podman.exe machine init` completes successfully:

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 79efdce5b126 done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
79efdce5b126608b8ced856442bc4b192b9ff85eec623d790a016d2212040cd9
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-amd64.vhdx: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start
```

I do think the https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/56e0f063bd280318a861d1c7f816160c31fdf7bb/pkg/machine/e2e/machine_test.go#L125 condition is clearly wrong. But, also, AFAICS, it “only” triggers a `Printf`, not (directly?) a test failure.

The error message `failed to close destination file` can be found in the CI logs of [other PRs too](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5104936988442624) so it's not related to this PR. 

I am going to do a rebase to re-trigger the CI.
